summary: The Docker-based reproduction clones Decidim `v0.30.4`, generates the upstream comments dummy app, and proves that the GraphQL mutation root can create a comment on an unpublished proposal that the query path refuses to expose.
expected_security_impact: The run should show that querying the unpublished proposal through GraphQL returns no commentable object while the mutation-root `commentable(...){ addComment(...) }` call still creates a comment on that unpublished proposal.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` completed successfully and emitted `QUERY_REJECTED_MUTATION_CREATED_COMMENT`. The proof JSON recorded `query_visible=false`, `comment_delta=1`, `mutation_response.commentable.addComment.id=1`, `mutation_response.commentable.addComment.body="evohunt unpublished proposal comment"`, and `proposal_published=false`.
verification_method: The reproduction generates `decidim-comments/spec/decidim_dummy_app` with Decidim's own test-app task, then executes a one-off RSpec proof that 1) queries the unpublished proposal through `Decidim::Api::QueryType` and expects `commentable` to be `nil`, and 2) executes the mutation-root `commentable(...){ addComment(...) }` path and expects the stored comment count to increase by one for the same unpublished proposal.
evidence_produced_by_run: The successful run emitted the `QUERY_REJECTED_MUTATION_CREATED_COMMENT` marker after the one-off RSpec proof completed.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
